title: POST /v1/responses passthrough (OpenAI family)
body:
## Goal
Add OpenAI Responses API create passthrough so clients using the Responses surface (agents, multi-step tools, structured outputs) can hit the gateway without dialect translation.

## Context
Today the gateway only serves chat (`POST /v1/chat/completions`), Anthropic messages, Google generateContent, and OpenAI-family image/video media. OpenAI (and several `openai_compat` hosts such as xAI) expose `POST /v1/responses`. This is same-family wire traffic: model rewrite + auth + one usage event, no canonical translation required for v1.

Module: `github.com/inja-online/llm-gateway`. Follow passthrough patterns in `proxy/openai_in.go` and `proxy/media.go`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /v1/responses` is registered on the gateway mux.
- [ ] Resolves `model` via existing `aliases` / `provider/model` / `defaults.openai_dialect`.
- [ ] Only routes to `kind: openai` or `kind: openai_compat` providers; other kinds return dialect-shaped 4xx without calling upstream.
- [ ] Rewrites JSON `model` to upstream model id; forwards Authorization (or `api_key_env` replacement) like chat.
- [ ] Non-streaming success and error bodies are forwarded with status and Content-Type preserved.
- [ ] Exactly one `hooks.UsageEvent` per request (`modality` appropriate — prefer `text` or document a new value if needed; default `text` unless design already extended).
- [ ] Token usage extracted from Responses usage fields when present (`estimated=false`); otherwise `estimated=true`.
- [ ] Air-gapped tests with `httptest` fake upstream: happy path, missing model, wrong provider kind, upstream 4xx/5xx.
- [ ] README HTTP API table updated.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer a dedicated handler (e.g. `handleResponses`) sharing exchange/auth helpers with OpenAI chat.
- Do not invent Anthropic/Google Responses twins in this issue.
- Preserve unknown JSON fields on request/response (passthrough map or raw body after model rewrite).
- Upstream path: `{base_url}/responses` (base_url already includes `/v1` for OpenAI).
- Streaming is a separate issue; this issue may reject `stream:true` with a clear error **or** implement basic SSE only if trivial — prefer non-stream first, link streaming issue.

## Out of scope
- Cross-dialect translation of Responses ↔ Messages / generateContent.
- Stored response GET/DELETE lifecycle (separate issue).
- Realtime / WebSocket.
- Background mode polling semantics beyond passthrough of create response.

## Related
- Responses streaming semantic events passthrough
- GET/DELETE /v1/responses/{id}
- Forward OpenAI-Organization / OpenAI-Project headers
- Rate-limit header passthrough policy

title: Responses streaming semantic events passthrough
body:
## Goal
Passthrough SSE (or HTTP chunked event stream) for `POST /v1/responses` with `stream: true`, preserving OpenAI Responses semantic event types byte-faithfully while metering usage.

## Context
Responses streaming uses typed events (`response.created`, `response.output_text.delta`, `response.completed`, etc.), not Chat Completions `data: {choices…}` chunks. Existing `passthroughStream` in `proxy/exchange.go` is Chat/Messages oriented for usage extraction; Responses needs its own usage extractor that scans for completed/failed events with usage objects.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `stream: true` on `POST /v1/responses` upgrades upstream request correctly (Accept / stream flag as OpenAI expects).
- [ ] Gateway relays stream bytes without re-encoding event boundaries incorrectly.
- [ ] Client disconnect cancels upstream context.
- [ ] One usage event at stream end; tokens from final usage-bearing event when present.
- [ ] Upstream error before stream start returns OpenAI error envelope.
- [ ] Hermetic tests with scripted multi-event SSE bodies; assert client receives identical framing for passthrough path.
- [ ] Document any known silent field drops (none expected on same-family passthrough).

## Implementation notes
- Reuse `internal/sse` line scanning where possible; do not parse into IR unless needed for usage.
- Keep `ttft_ms` if first data event timing is available (parity with chat streams).
- openai_compat hosts that partially implement Responses: passthrough still; capability failures are upstream 404s unless we later add a capability flag.

## Out of scope
- Translating Responses stream events into Anthropic/Google stream formats.
- WebSocket Realtime.
- Reordering or coalescing events.

## Related
- POST /v1/responses passthrough (OpenAI family)
- Rate-limit header passthrough policy

title: GET/DELETE /v1/responses/{id} if applicable
body:
## Goal
Support OpenAI Responses resource lifecycle endpoints when the configured OpenAI-family upstream implements them: retrieve and delete a stored response by id.

## Context
OpenAI Responses may be stored and later fetched/deleted (`GET/DELETE /v1/responses/{response_id}`). The gateway is stateless; it must not store responses itself — only proxy. Routing is harder than create because there may be no `model` in the path; need an explicit provider selector.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `GET /v1/responses/{id}` and `DELETE /v1/responses/{id}` registered.
- [ ] Provider resolution via query `?provider=` **or** header **or** `defaults.openai_dialect` (document precedence; mirror video GET pattern where possible).
- [ ] Rejects non-OpenAI-family providers with clear 4xx.
- [ ] Forwards path and query string; rewrites auth; does not invent local storage.
- [ ] Usage events: operational (tokens 0, estimated true) or skip if consistent with other GET probes — document chosen rule (prefer one event with zero tokens for observability).
- [ ] Air-gapped tests for resolve-provider, 200 body passthrough, 404 upstream, missing provider when no default.
- [ ] README documents that gateway does not retain response bodies across requests.

## Implementation notes
- Align with `handleVideosGet` provider selection style.
- If OpenAI deprecates or gates these routes, gate behind docs note; still implement passthrough for compat hosts that support them.

## Out of scope
- Listing responses, cancel, or input items subresources unless trivial and documented.
- Multi-provider fan-out search for an id.

## Related
- POST /v1/responses passthrough (OpenAI family)
- Aggregate /v1/models capability flags from providers

title: OpenAI Realtime WebSocket upgrade /v1/realtime passthrough
body:
## Goal
Accept OpenAI Realtime WebSocket upgrades at `GET /v1/realtime` (with Upgrade) and passthrough-bidirectionally to OpenAI or capable `openai_compat` upstreams with auth rewrite.

## Context
Design spec §5.5 / PR5: realtime only for **real** protocols. No Anthropic WS. Capability-centric: `capabilities.realtime` default native for `openai`, opt-in for `openai_compat`, unsupported for anthropic/google on this path (Google uses Live path).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] HTTP Upgrade handshake on `/v1/realtime` with required OpenAI Realtime headers/query (`model`, etc.) accepted per current OpenAI Realtime client expectations.
- [ ] Capability check before dial; unsupported → dialect-shaped error **before** upgrade when possible.
- [ ] Dials upstream WS using provider `base_url` (http→ws / https→wss mapping) + auth (`api_key_env` or client key).
- [ ] Bidirectional frame passthrough until either side closes.
- [ ] Client abort and upstream failure both terminate cleanly without leaking goroutines (race-clean tests).
- [ ] Hermetic tests with fake WS peer (`internal/testutil` / httptest hijack); no public network.
- [ ] README + example config notes for Realtime.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer stdlib hijack + minimal framing; one small WS dep only if justified (design Q2).
- Package layout target: `proxy/realtime/` accept, dial, passthrough.
- Model resolution: query param and/or first `session.update` — document chosen approach; at minimum rewrite auth and model in session config when present in query.
- Session limits and usage events are separate issues but wire hooks so they can plug in.

## Out of scope
- OpenAI Realtime ↔ Google Live bridge (epic).
- Anthropic Realtime dialect.
- Audio persistence to disk.

## Related
- Realtime session limits from config
- Realtime usage event on session end
- Optional Realtime ↔ Live bridge IR
- Google Live BidiGenerateContent WebSocket passthrough

title: Google Live BidiGenerateContent WebSocket passthrough
body:
## Goal
Passthrough Google Live / BidiGenerateContent WebSocket sessions on the documented Google-shaped path so native Gemini Live clients work through the gateway.

## Context
Design: Google Live ingress is first-class; passthrough to `kind: google` (and later bridge to OpenAI Realtime). Path should match public Google Live docs under `/v1beta/...` (confirm exact path at implement time, e.g. models live bidirectional generate content).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Documented Live WS route registered and versioned in README.
- [ ] Auth: `x-goog-api-key` or `api_key_env` rewrite consistent with HTTP Google egress.
- [ ] Model in path/query resolved via aliases / `provider/model` / `defaults.google_dialect`.
- [ ] Capability `realtime` required; fail closed for providers without it.
- [ ] Bidirectional passthrough; clean shutdown on either side close.
- [ ] Air-gapped fake WS tests; golden minimal session open/close frames optional.
- [ ] No invented Anthropic WS mirror.

## Implementation notes
- Share session registry / limit plumbing with OpenAI Realtime passthrough.
- Keep Google error/close codes visible to client where protocol allows.

## Out of scope
- Bridge to OpenAI Realtime (separate epic).
- Translating Live to HTTP generateContent.

## Related
- OpenAI Realtime WebSocket upgrade /v1/realtime passthrough
- Realtime session limits from config
- Realtime usage event on session end
- Optional Realtime ↔ Live bridge IR

title: Realtime session limits from config (max_sessions, max_session_minutes)
body:
## Goal
Enforce configurable realtime concurrency and duration limits so a single gateway process cannot unbounded-accept WS sessions.

## Context
Design §10.2 / §13:
```yaml
realtime:
  max_sessions: 1024
  max_session_minutes: 60
```
Process-local counters are correct (gateway is stateless multi-replica; each replica enforces its own cap).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Config struct + YAML parse + validation (positive ints; sensible defaults when section omitted).
- [ ] New session rejected when `max_sessions` active sessions reached (clear error before or during upgrade).
- [ ] Active session force-closed with protocol error/close when `max_session_minutes` exceeded.
- [ ] Counters decrement on all exit paths (client abort, upstream error, limit, shutdown drain).
- [ ] Shutdown drain (30s existing spirit) closes WS sessions with error frame.
- [ ] Unit tests for counter edge cases; no `time.Sleep` flakiness — use fake clock or injectable timer.
- [ ] `gateway.example.yaml` documents the section.

## Implementation notes
- Atomic or mutex-guarded session map keyed by request/session id.
- Limits apply to both OpenAI Realtime and Google Live passthrough (and later bridge).

## Out of scope
- Cluster-wide distributed session limits.
- Per-API-key quotas.

## Related
- OpenAI Realtime WebSocket upgrade /v1/realtime passthrough
- Google Live BidiGenerateContent WebSocket passthrough
- Realtime usage event on session end

title: Realtime usage event on session end
body:
## Goal
Emit exactly one `hooks.UsageEvent` when a realtime WebSocket session ends, with modality/transport/media fields suitable for external billing.

## Context
Design §4.7 / §11: one usage unit per realtime **session end** (not per frame). `modality=realtime`, `transport=websocket`, `media.unit_kind=session_minute` (and audio seconds when known). Mid-session samples out of scope for v1.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Event emitted once on normal close, client abort, upstream failure, and duration limit.
- [ ] Fields: `request_id`, `dialect_in`, `provider`, `model`/`upstream_model`, `modality`, `transport`, `latency_ms` (session duration), `status` (`ok`|`client_abort`|`upstream_error`|…), `key_hash`, optional `media`.
- [ ] Prefer upstream-reported usage if available in protocol events; else `estimated=true` with session minutes derived from wall duration.
- [ ] No usage event for failed capability check that never opens a session (HTTP error only).
- [ ] Hook tests with collector; assert single emit under abort and success.

## Implementation notes
- Extend `hooks.UsageEvent` if fields already planned in multimodal core (`media`, `modality`, `transport`).
- Never log raw audio; never put keys in events.

## Out of scope
- Mid-session metering samples.
- In-gateway currency pricing.

## Related
- Realtime session limits from config
- OpenAI Realtime WebSocket upgrade /v1/realtime passthrough
- Optional Realtime ↔ Live bridge IR

title: Epic: Optional Realtime ↔ Live bridge IR + subtasks
body:
## Goal
Define and implement an optional protocol bridge between OpenAI Realtime and Google Live using a dialect-neutral realtime IR, so a client speaking one live protocol can reach an upstream speaking the other.

## Context
Design D3 / §7.3 / §8.6 / PR6. Bridge is best-effort for tools and audio formats; undocumented fields go to `Extra` or explicit drop lists with metrics/tests. **No Anthropic WebSocket dialect.**

This epic should be split into subtasks (file as child issues when implementing):
1. `canonical/realtime.go` IR types (session config + core events).
2. OpenAI Realtime event → IR mapper + reverse.
3. Google Live message → IR mapper + reverse.
4. Audio format conversion best-effort (PCM rates) with documented limitations.
5. Tool/function-call mapping best-effort.
6. Proxy bridge session runner (two WS legs + IR).
7. Golden fixture sequences both directions.
8. Error `unsupported_realtime_bridge` when pair not supported.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Capability path: OpenAI RT ingress → Google Live egress and reverse when configured providers support realtime.
- [ ] Same-protocol continues to use pure passthrough (no IR tax).
- [ ] Golden in/out event sequence tests air-gapped.
- [ ] Explicit drop-list tests for unmapped fields.
- [ ] One usage event at session end (shared with passthrough metering).
- [ ] README documents bridge limitations (audio, tools, VAD).
- [ ] Subtasks linked from this epic description or checklist.

## Implementation notes
- Fail closed with `unsupported_realtime_bridge` rather than half-bridging silently.
- Do not write session audio to disk.
- Prefer table-driven golden JSON under `testdata/fixtures/`.

## Out of scope
- Inventing Anthropic live WS.
- Perfect lossless parity for every vendor event.
- Multiparty conferences / SIP.

## Related
- OpenAI Realtime WebSocket upgrade /v1/realtime passthrough
- Google Live BidiGenerateContent WebSocket passthrough
- Package split canonical/{chat,image,video,audio,realtime}.go
- Design-spec fixture golden files for media contracts

title: OpenAI Files API proxy (basic)
body:
## Goal
Basic OpenAI Files API passthrough so agents and Assistants/Responses workflows that upload files can use the gateway as base URL.

## Context
Files are binary/multipart; gateway must not persist uploads beyond the request. Routing needs provider selection without always having a model field.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] At least: `POST /v1/files` (upload multipart), `GET /v1/files`, `GET /v1/files/{id}`, `DELETE /v1/files/{id}`, and `GET /v1/files/{id}/content` if low-cost.
- [ ] Provider via `?provider=` / default openai dialect / optional header; documented.
- [ ] Multipart body forwarded with Content-Type boundary intact.
- [ ] Auth rewrite; OpenAI-family only.
- [ ] Usage: operational events or none for pure file I/O — document; prefer one event with zero tokens for mutations.
- [ ] Size limit respects global body limit (review if 32 MiB is too small; document).
- [ ] Air-gapped multipart tests; no disk spool of file content except stdlib temp if required then deleted.
- [ ] README API table + security note: files live on upstream, not gateway.

## Implementation notes
- Do not log file bytes.
- Content download should stream through without full buffering when possible.

## Out of scope
- Cross-provider file migration.
- Anthropic Files (separate issue).
- Virus scanning.

## Related
- Anthropic Files API proxy (beta headers forward)
- Security review of media/audio multipart
- Request body size / streaming limits review

title: Anthropic Files API proxy (beta headers forward)
body:
## Goal
Proxy Anthropic Files API endpoints with required beta headers forwarded/merged correctly for Claude tool and code workflows.

## Context
Anthropic Files often require `anthropic-beta` values and `anthropic-version`. Current Anthropic egress sets a fixed `anthropic-version` on chat; Files needs careful header policy so client beta flags are not stripped.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Routes mirror Anthropic Files HTTP surface used by official SDK (list/upload/retrieve/delete as applicable).
- [ ] Forwards `anthropic-version` from client when present; default when absent.
- [ ] Forwards `anthropic-beta` (including comma-separated / multiple values per verified policy).
- [ ] Provider resolution to `kind: anthropic` only (or anthropic-family).
- [ ] Multipart upload fidelity; no gateway persistence.
- [ ] Hermetic tests asserting beta headers reach fake upstream.
- [ ] README documents beta header behavior for Files.

## Implementation notes
- Coordinate with “Forward anthropic-beta unknown values (verify)” so policy is shared.
- Fail closed if routed to non-Anthropic provider.

## Out of scope
- Translating OpenAI Files ↔ Anthropic Files.
- Batch API (separate).

## Related
- Forward anthropic-beta unknown values (verify)
- OpenAI Files API proxy (basic)
- Anthropic Batch API proxy

title: Anthropic Batch API proxy
body:
## Goal
Passthrough Anthropic Message Batches API so bulk jobs can be submitted and polled through the gateway.

## Context
Batches are long-lived server-side jobs on Anthropic. Gateway remains stateless: create/list/get/cancel/results are pure proxy. Beta headers often required.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Proxy core Batch routes (create, retrieve, list, cancel, results stream/JSON as per current Anthropic API).
- [ ] Model rewrite inside batch request payloads when models are gateway public ids (`provider/model`, aliases).
- [ ] Auth + `anthropic-version` + `anthropic-beta` handling consistent with Files/chat policy.
- [ ] Usage events: document (create vs poll); recommend event on create with estimated tokens if available, light operational events on poll.
- [ ] Air-gapped tests for model rewrite in nested batch JSON.
- [ ] README section: batches are upstream-owned; gateway does not store results.

## Implementation notes
- Nested model fields may appear per request in the batch — implement robust JSON walk or targeted rewrite for known schema paths; test heavily.
- Do not translate batch contents to OpenAI batch format.

## Out of scope
- OpenAI Batch API (unless trivial follow-up).
- Gateway-side job scheduling.

## Related
- Anthropic Files API proxy (beta headers forward)
- Forward anthropic-beta unknown values (verify)

title: Moderations POST /v1/moderations passthrough
body:
## Goal
Passthrough `POST /v1/moderations` to OpenAI-family providers for safety classifiers used by apps and agents.

## Context
Simple JSON API; same auth as Chat Completions. Useful for OpenAI SDK compatibility when base_url points at gateway.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Route registered; model rewrite when model present; default provider routing works for bare models.
- [ ] OpenAI-family only; clear error otherwise.
- [ ] Response passthrough; one usage event (tokens if any, else estimated zero tokens).
- [ ] Tests with fake upstream.
- [ ] README API table row.

## Implementation notes
- Keep handler small; share media/openai helpers.

## Out of scope
- Multi-provider moderation ensembles.
- Anthropic/Google native moderation twins.

## Related
- POST /v1/responses passthrough (OpenAI family)
- Rate-limit header passthrough policy

title: Conversations API: document skip or minimal stub
body:
## Goal
Decide and document whether OpenAI Conversations API is supported, explicitly skipped, or stubbed, so SDK users are not surprised by 404s.

## Context
OpenAI has been evolving Conversations / stateful threads alongside Responses. Gateway is stateless and prefers passthrough of real upstream resources. Full Conversations support may be low ROI if Responses + Files cover agent needs.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Written decision in README roadmap or HTTP API section: **skip** | **stub 501** | **passthrough**.
- [ ] If skip: document recommended alternative (Responses, client-side state).
- [ ] If stub: return clear OpenAI-shaped error (`not_implemented` / similar) with message pointing to docs.
- [ ] If passthrough: implement minimal create/get routes with provider selection (promote to feature issue and link).
- [ ] Issue closes only when docs (and stub/code if chosen) land.

## Implementation notes
- Default recommendation: **document skip** unless product prioritizes it; avoid half-implemented state APIs.

## Out of scope
- Building a gateway-side conversation store.

## Related
- POST /v1/responses passthrough (OpenAI family)
- OpenAI Files API proxy (basic)

title: Forward OpenAI-Organization and OpenAI-Project headers
body:
## Goal
Forward `OpenAI-Organization` and `OpenAI-Project` (and current OpenAI org/project header spellings) from client to OpenAI-family upstreams so multi-project accounts work through the gateway.

## Context
Many enterprise OpenAI setups require org/project headers. Stripping them causes mysterious 401/403 upstream. Chat, Responses, Files, Moderations, Realtime should share one allowlist policy.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Headers forwarded on OpenAI-family egress for HTTP routes that use client credentials.
- [ ] When `api_key_env` replaces the key, still forward org/project if client sent them (unless config opts out — document default on).
- [ ] Headers **not** sent to Anthropic/Google kinds.
- [ ] Tests assert presence/absence per provider kind.
- [ ] README Auth section mentions org/project forwarding.

## Implementation notes
- Centralize hop-by-hop vs end-to-end header filter; avoid ad-hoc copies per handler.
- Case-insensitive header names per HTTP rules.

## Out of scope
- Configuring default org/project in YAML (optional follow-up).
- Azure OpenAI deployment headers (unless already needed for openai_compat).

## Related
- Rate-limit header passthrough policy
- POST /v1/responses passthrough (OpenAI family)

title: Forward anthropic-beta unknown values (verify + harden)
body:
## Goal
Verify current Anthropic header handling and ensure unknown/new `anthropic-beta` values are forwarded rather than dropped or overwritten, so new Anthropic betas work without gateway releases when possible.

## Context
Anthropic frequently gates features behind `anthropic-beta`. Gateway may currently force `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01` and not merge client betas on all paths. Files/Batch/agent features depend on correct beta passthrough.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Audit all Anthropic ingress→egress paths (messages, count_tokens, future files/batch) for beta/version behavior.
- [ ] Document normative policy: client `anthropic-beta` forwarded; multiple headers/comma-lists preserved per HTTP semantics chosen.
- [ ] Gateway does not require an allowlist of beta strings for passthrough (unknown values ok).
- [ ] If gateway must set a beta for a translated feature, merge rather than replace when possible; document forced betas.
- [ ] Tests cover: client beta only; none; multiple values; version default vs client override.
- [ ] README / CONTRIBUTING note for future modalities.

## Implementation notes
- Fix any path that silently strips betas.
- Coordinate with Files and Batch proxy issues.

## Out of scope
- Implementing every beta feature’s translation semantics.

## Related
- Anthropic Files API proxy (beta headers forward)
- Anthropic Batch API proxy

title: Rate-limit header passthrough policy
body:
## Goal
Define and implement a consistent policy for forwarding upstream rate-limit and request-id headers to clients across dialects.

## Context
SDKs and operators rely on `x-ratelimit-*`, `retry-after`, `anthropic-ratelimit-*`, `x-request-id`, Google headers, etc. Gateway may currently drop non-essential response headers on some paths.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Written policy table: which request headers inbound are forwarded; which response headers outbound are forwarded; which are hop-by-hop stripped.
- [ ] Implementation matches policy for OpenAI chat/media/responses, Anthropic messages, Google generateContent, and streams where applicable.
- [ ] Gateway may add its own `x-request-id` / correlation id without removing upstream request ids if both exist (document).
- [ ] Tests for representative headers per dialect.
- [ ] README or docs section for operators.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer allowlist of safe forward headers over full copy of all upstream headers.
- Never forward `Set-Cookie` or mid-proxy auth challenges that leak upstream topology unexpectedly — call out in policy.

## Out of scope
- Gateway-enforced rate limiting (token bucket).
- Cross-provider rate limit aggregation.

## Related
- Forward OpenAI-Organization / OpenAI-Project headers
- POST /v1/responses passthrough (OpenAI family)

title: Anthropic gateway POST /v1/images generate contract
body:
## Goal
Implement the gateway-defined Anthropic-shaped image generation contract `POST /v1/images` (not `/v1/images/generations`) requiring `anthropic-version`, with Anthropic error envelope.

## Context
Design §5.2 / §9.2 / PR1. Anthropic has no public image gen twin; hybrid decision invents a stable Gateway Media Contract v1. Path disambiguation: OpenAI uses `/v1/images/generations`; Anthropic media uses `/v1/images` + `anthropic-version`.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `POST /v1/images` requires `anthropic-version`; missing → 400 Anthropic-shaped.
- [ ] Request fields per design sketch: `model`, `prompt`, `n`, `size`, `response_format`, etc.
- [ ] Routes to capable providers (`openai`/`openai_compat`/`google` with image_gen); anthropic provider → `unsupported_provider_capability`.
- [ ] Same-family OpenAI egress builds OpenAI images wire; Google egress uses native/translated path.
- [ ] Response Anthropic-gateway shape (`type`, `data[].b64_json`, usage…).
- [ ] If `anthropic-version` is sent to `/v1/images/generations` → 400 clear message to use `POST /v1/images`.
- [ ] Matrix tests + fixtures; usage event `modality=image_gen`.
- [ ] Document in README Media Contract v1 (link docs issue).

## Implementation notes
- Replace passthrough-only limitation gradually; keep OpenAI paths working.
- Canonical image types may land in package-split issue; can start with focused types.

## Out of scope
- Image edits (separate issue).
- Realtime.

## Related
- Anthropic gateway image edits
- Cross-dialect image gen translation canonical
- Document Gateway Media Contract v1 in README
- Design-spec fixture golden files for media contracts

title: Anthropic gateway image edits
body:
## Goal
Implement `POST /v1/images/edits` Anthropic-gateway contract (anthropic-version, snake_case, Anthropic errors) with translation to capable egress providers.

## Context
Design §5.2 pairs generate + edits for Anthropic-shaped media. Edits may be JSON+base64 or multipart depending on contract finalization; fixtures are authoritative.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Route requires `anthropic-version`.
- [ ] Supports source image + prompt (+ mask if in contract).
- [ ] Capability-checked routing; fail closed for anthropic upstream.
- [ ] Multipart and/or JSON accepted as specified; round-trip tests.
- [ ] Usage `image_gen` with media units.
- [ ] Fixtures under `testdata/fixtures/`.

## Implementation notes
- Align field names with generate contract for client simplicity.
- Reuse canonical image `mode=edit`.

## Out of scope
- Variations-only endpoint if not in Anthropic contract.
- Video edits.

## Related
- Anthropic gateway POST /v1/images generate contract
- Cross-dialect image gen translation canonical
- Multipart + binary response fidelity for TTS

title: Google POST :generateImages or :predict Imagen path
body:
## Goal
Expose Google-shaped image generation ingress `POST /v1beta/models/{model}:generateImages` (or documented Imagen `:predict` path if that is the current public twin) with Google error/request shapes.

## Context
Design §5.2. Model-in-path, `x-goog-api-key`. May passthrough to `kind: google` or translate to OpenAI images egress.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Route registered consistently with existing `{action}` Google mux pattern or dedicated pattern — no collision with generateContent.
- [ ] Parses Google-shaped request; capability check `image_gen`.
- [ ] Passthrough to Google when provider kind google; translate to OpenAI family when routed there.
- [ ] Errors use Google envelope on this ingress.
- [ ] Matrix tests: google→google, google→openai, openai_compat cap off deny.
- [ ] README path table updated.

## Implementation notes
- Confirm final Google method name against current Gemini/Imagen docs at implement time; encode in fixtures.
- Avoid breaking generateContent routing.

## Out of scope
- Video LRO (separate).
- Anthropic contract (separate).

## Related
- Cross-dialect image gen translation canonical
- Google video generate + LRO poll native paths

title: Google video generate + LRO poll native paths
body:
## Goal
Implement Google-native video generation create + long-running operation poll paths through the gateway.

## Context
Design §5.3: `POST /v1beta/models/{model}:generateVideos`, `GET /v1beta/videos/{name}` (operation poll). Canonical job model maps provider job/operation IDs. OpenAI `/v1/videos` already exists as passthrough.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Create + poll routes work for Google ingress dialect.
- [ ] Capability `video_gen`; fail closed when unsupported.
- [ ] Poll does not require client to know upstream id format beyond create response.
- [ ] Usage: create bills media units; poll operational with zero media units (design Q3 default).
- [ ] Translate to OpenAI video jobs when egress is OpenAI-family (if translation issue landed or same PR stack).
- [ ] Air-gapped LRO fake: create returns op name; poll transitions queued→completed.
- [ ] README documents Google video paths vs OpenAI `/v1/videos`.

## Implementation notes
- Coordinate job id mapping with cross-dialect video translation issue.
- Do not block poll on gateway-side storage.

## Out of scope
- Downloading full video binaries into gateway memory beyond streaming proxy of result URLs/content if required by dialect.

## Related
- Cross-dialect video gen translation canonical
- Capability matrix e2e tests for all modality cells

title: Cross-dialect image gen translation canonical
body:
## Goal
Implement bidirectional image generation translation via canonical image request/response so any HTTP image ingress dialect can reach any capable egress.

## Context
Design §8.2 / PR1. Today image gen is OpenAI-compat passthrough only. Canonical fields: model, prompt, n, size, quality, style, response_format, mode, source images, mask, seed, Extra.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `canonical` image types used on cross-family paths only; same-family remains passthrough.
- [ ] Matrix cells from design §12.6 for `image_gen` green (including unsupported anthropic egress).
- [ ] openai_compat requires `capabilities.image_gen: true`.
- [ ] Drop-list / Extra behavior tested for unknown fields.
- [ ] Usage events include media size/units when known.
- [ ] Coverage gate maintained.

## Implementation notes
- Package split may move types to `canonical/image.go` (related issue).
- Prefer fixtures over ad-hoc large base64 (`YQ==`).

## Out of scope
- Video/audio canonical (separate).
- Perfect vendor parity for every quality enum.

## Related
- Anthropic gateway POST /v1/images generate contract
- Google POST :generateImages or :predict Imagen path
- Package split canonical/{chat,image,video,audio,realtime}.go

title: Cross-dialect video gen translation canonical
body:
## Goal
Implement canonical video job create/poll translation across OpenAI, Anthropic-gateway, and Google video dialects.

## Context
Design §8.3 / PR2. Request: model, prompt, duration, resolution, aspect, references, operation, job_id. Response: status enum, progress, result URLs/b64, errors.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Cross-family create+poll maps status enums correctly (`queued|processing|completed|failed`).
- [ ] Job ids round-trip in the dialect that created them.
- [ ] Capability matrix including openai_compat opt-in and anthropic capability deny.
- [ ] Usage policy: create vs poll documented and tested.
- [ ] Fixtures for each dialect wire shape.

## Implementation notes
- Anthropic video routes require anthropic-version and non-colliding paths per design §5.3.
- Avoid assuming OpenAI and Google id formats are interchangeable without mapping layer.

## Out of scope
- Realtime video streams.
- Gateway-side video transcoding.

## Related
- Google video generate + LRO poll native paths
- Capability matrix e2e tests for all modality cells
- Design-spec fixture golden files for media contracts

title: Google audio speech via generateContent modalities or dedicated path
body:
## Goal
Support Google-shaped TTS egress/ingress either via `generateContent` audio modalities or dedicated `POST /v1beta/models/{model}:generateSpeech` as locked in design §5.4 — implement the path that matches current Google public API and gateway contract.

## Context
Voice HTTP is new (PR3). OpenAI already will use `/v1/audio/speech`. Google needs a native-shaped route for multi-dialect symmetry.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Documented Google TTS route works end-to-end with fake upstream.
- [ ] Binary or JSON audio response per Google contract; Content-Type preserved on passthrough.
- [ ] Capability `audio_speech`; fail closed when off.
- [ ] Translation to OpenAI TTS egress when routed to openai/openai_compat.
- [ ] Usage: `modality=audio_speech`, media characters/minutes/format when known.
- [ ] README voice table updated.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer dedicated path if stable; if only modalities-on-generateContent exist, document dual-use carefully to avoid breaking chat.
- No disk writes of audio.

## Out of scope
- STT (can share PR but tracked with Anthropic audio issue as needed).
- Realtime WS audio.

## Related
- Anthropic gateway audio speech/transcribe contracts
- Multipart + binary response fidelity for TTS
- Cross-dialect image gen translation canonical

title: Anthropic gateway audio speech and transcribe contracts
body:
## Goal
Add gateway-defined Anthropic-shaped HTTP contracts for TTS and STT: `POST /v1/audio/speech` and `POST /v1/audio/transcriptions` (+ optional translations), requiring `anthropic-version`.

## Context
Design §5.4 / §9 hybrid. Anthropic clients get stable snake_case + Anthropic errors even though upstream is OpenAI/Google audio-capable providers.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Both speech and transcription routes require `anthropic-version`.
- [ ] STT accepts multipart and JSON+base64 where schema allows.
- [ ] TTS returns raw audio or JSON wrapper per contract; fidelity tests.
- [ ] Capability checks for `audio_speech` / `audio_transcribe`.
- [ ] Translation to OpenAI and Google egress.
- [ ] Usage events with media unit kinds (`audio_character`, `audio_minute`, etc.).
- [ ] Fixtures + matrix tests including unsupported anthropic provider egress.

## Implementation notes
- Path collision with OpenAI `/v1/audio/*`: disambiguate by `anthropic-version` header rules (document; mirror images policy).
- Optional translations endpoint if low cost.

## Out of scope
- Anthropic Realtime WS.
- Speaker diarization guarantees.

## Related
- Google audio speech via generateContent modalities or dedicated path
- Multipart + binary response fidelity for TTS
- Document Gateway Media Contract v1 in README

title: Capability matrix e2e tests for all modality cells
body:
## Goal
Encode design §12.6 modality×dialect×provider-kind matrix as automated air-gapped e2e/proxy tests that fail closed on unsupported cells and succeed on supported ones.

## Context
Matrix is the routing truth. Without it, capability regressions slip in. Includes image_gen, video_gen, audio_speech, audio_transcribe, and realtime rows as those land.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Table-driven matrix helper in `internal/testutil` or `proxy` tests.
- [ ] Each HTTP modality covered for dialects openai/anthropic/google × kinds openai/openai_compat/anthropic/google with expected 200 or 4xx capability.
- [ ] openai_compat default media off asserted (no upstream call).
- [ ] Fake upstream `t.Fatal` on unexpected calls for deny cells.
- [ ] Realtime subset when WS available: RT↔RT passthrough, anthropic deny.
- [ ] Runs under `go test ./... -race` offline.
- [ ] Document how to add a cell in CONTRIBUTING modality guide (link).

## Implementation notes
- Build incrementally; issue can land skeleton first then fill cells per modality PR.
- No `time.Sleep`; no live tags in CI.

## Out of scope
- Live provider tests in CI.

## Related
- Cross-dialect image gen translation canonical
- Cross-dialect video gen translation canonical
- CONTRIBUTING guide for adding a modality
- End-to-end compatibility matrix doc in docs/

title: Package split canonical/{chat,image,video,audio,realtime}.go
body:
## Goal
Split `canonical` package from a chat-centric single file into modality-focused files matching design §7.1 without changing public behavior.

## Context
Target layout:
```
canonical/
  chat.go
  image.go
  video.go
  audio.go
  realtime.go
  capability.go  # optional shared constants
```
Enables cleaner reviews for media PRs and matches capability-centric architecture.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Files exist; types live in logical files; package name remains `canonical`.
- [ ] All existing tests green; no API breakage for importers.
- [ ] Chat audio input block (`BlockAudio`) lives in chat or audio with clear ownership.
- [ ] README/CONTRIBUTING layout table updated.
- [ ] No behavior change (refactor-only commits preferred).

## Implementation notes
- Do this early in M4 to reduce merge pain, or at end of PR0 skeleton — coordinate with multimodal core.
- Avoid circular deps with proxy.

## Out of scope
- New translation logic (belongs in feature issues).

## Related
- Cross-dialect image gen translation canonical
- Epic: Optional Realtime ↔ Live bridge IR + subtasks
- CONTRIBUTING guide for adding a modality

title: Design-spec fixture golden files for media contracts
body:
## Goal
Populate `testdata/fixtures/` with golden HTTP (and WS) media contract samples per design §12.4 so wire shapes are authoritative and reviewable.

## Context
Naming: `{dialect}_{modality}_{case}.json`. Tiny base64 only. `testdata/fixtures/README.md` explains offline production. Fixtures lock Gateway Media Contract v1.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Fixtures for openai/anthropic-gateway/google × image_gen (generate minimum); edits if applicable.
- [ ] Video create/poll samples per dialect.
- [ ] Audio speech/transcribe samples; multipart STT generated in-test preferred.
- [ ] Error envelope samples per dialect.
- [ ] Optional realtime event sequences directory for bridge.
- [ ] README in fixtures documents naming and regeneration policy (`fixtures` build tag must not hit network).
- [ ] At least one test per fixture file ensuring parse/serialize stability.

## Implementation notes
- Sanitize any captured live fixtures; CI only reads committed files.
- Keep blobs tiny (`YQ==`).

## Out of scope
- Full production media samples.
- Live regeneration in CI.

## Related
- Document Gateway Media Contract v1 in README
- Capability matrix e2e tests for all modality cells
- Epic: Optional Realtime ↔ Live bridge IR + subtasks

title: Multipart + binary response fidelity for TTS
body:
## Goal
Guarantee that multipart STT requests and binary TTS responses pass through (or translate) without corruption of boundaries, Content-Type, or audio bytes.

## Context
Design §12.8. OpenAI speech returns raw audio; translation paths may re-encode only when dialect requires. Multipart peek for model is best-effort (existing media.go pattern).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] In-memory multipart builder tests for STT upload fields (file, model, language).
- [ ] TTS success asserts Content-Type and raw bytes equality with fake upstream on passthrough.
- [ ] Translation path tests document any re-encoding; default avoid re-encode.
- [ ] Body size limit behavior tested near boundary for multipart.
- [ ] No full-buffer surprises for moderately large audio within limit (stream when possible).

## Implementation notes
- Shared helpers in `internal/testutil`.
- Race-clean.

## Out of scope
- Perceptual audio quality metrics.
- Real microphone capture.

## Related
- Anthropic gateway audio speech and transcribe contracts
- Google audio speech via generateContent modalities or dedicated path
- Security review of media/audio multipart
- Request body size / streaming limits review

title: Document Gateway Media Contract v1 in README
body:
## Goal
Publish Gateway Media Contract v1 in README (and link design spec) covering Anthropic-shaped and Google-shaped media routes, path disambiguation, headers, and error codes.

## Context
Design §9: stable & versioned contracts; fixtures authoritative. Operators and SDK users need a single public table without reading the full design doc.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] README sections: Image, Video, Voice tables for three dialects.
- [ ] Path disambiguation rules (OpenAI vs Anthropic image paths; anthropic-version requirements).
- [ ] Logical gateway error codes mapped to dialect envelopes (`unsupported_provider_capability`, `invalid_media_request`, …).
- [ ] Capability defaults table for provider kinds (openai_compat opt-in).
- [ ] Link to `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-18-multimodal-gateway-design.md`.
- [ ] Example curl snippets for Anthropic-gateway image and OpenAI image.
- [ ] Changelog note when contract ships (coordinate M5 changelog issue).

## Implementation notes
- Keep README accurate to implemented surface only; mark “planned” clearly if docs land early.

## Out of scope
- Full OpenAPI PDF.
- Pricing tables.

## Related
- Design-spec fixture golden files for media contracts
- Changelog + versioning for API surface expansion
- End-to-end compatibility matrix doc in docs/

title: Optional gateway edge auth (API key gate)
body:
## Goal
Add optional edge authentication so the gateway can reject unauthenticated callers when deployed on semi-trusted networks, without becoming a full IAM product.

## Context
README roadmap and SECURITY.md: gateway currently does not authenticate clients; keys are forwarded upstream. Design allows optional edge auth later. Keep it simple: static API keys or bearer tokens in config/env.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Config e.g. `edge_auth.enabled` + `edge_auth.keys_env` or hashed keys list — final schema documented.
- [ ] When disabled (default), behavior unchanged.
- [ ] When enabled, missing/invalid client edge credential → 401 dialect-neutral or OpenAI-shaped for unknown routes; documented.
- [ ] Edge auth distinct from upstream provider key (`api_key_env` still works).
- [ ] `/healthz` remains unauthenticated for probes (document).
- [ ] Tests for on/off/wrong key; no secrets in fixtures.
- [ ] SECURITY.md + README Auth section updated.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer constant-time compare for secrets.
- Do not log edge keys; key_hash may use edge key or upstream key — document which.

## Out of scope
- OAuth/OIDC, mTLS (document as external), per-tenant RBAC, rate cards.

## Related
- Security review of media/audio multipart
- Provider health check optional

title: Vertex AI ADC / service-account auth helper
body:
## Goal
Help operators call Vertex AI / Google endpoints with application default credentials or service-account JSON instead of only API keys.

## Context
Roadmap item. Current Google auth is `x-goog-api-key`. Enterprise Vertex often uses OAuth2 access tokens from ADC/SA.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Config option on google (or new) provider kind/fields: e.g. `auth: api_key|adc|service_account` + `service_account_file` / env.
- [ ] Access tokens obtained and refreshed without blocking all requests on a single hung refresh (document concurrency).
- [ ] Air-gapped tests with fake token source interface (no real GCP).
- [ ] Example YAML for Vertex base URLs.
- [ ] README + SECURITY notes on mounting SA JSON read-only.

## Implementation notes
- Optional dependency on Google auth libs only if justified; prefer small interface + env `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS` integration tested with fakes.
- Do not break existing API key path.

## Out of scope
- Full multi-cloud secret manager integration.
- AWS Bedrock.

## Related
- Qwen regional base URL config helpers
- Optional gateway edge auth (API key gate)

title: OpenRouter provider routing + plugins docs and passthrough tests
body:
## Goal
Document OpenRouter-specific usage through the gateway (routing, provider preferences, plugins headers/body fields) and add passthrough tests ensuring extra fields/headers are not stripped.

## Context
`gateway.example.yaml` already includes `openrouter` openai_compat. OpenRouter uses extra headers (`HTTP-Referer`, `X-Title`) and body fields for provider routing/plugins.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] README or `docs/` section: base_url, auth, example model ids (`openrouter/...`).
- [ ] Document which OpenRouter headers are forwarded (align with header policy issue).
- [ ] Tests: extra JSON fields on chat completions survive passthrough; optional headers forwarded.
- [ ] Note capabilities opt-in for media if users expect image models.
- [ ] Example snippet in `gateway.example.yaml` comments.

## Implementation notes
- Passthrough-first; no OpenRouter-specific IR.

## Out of scope
- Implementing OpenRouter’s full provider ranking engine in-gateway.

## Related
- Rate-limit header passthrough policy
- Aggregate /v1/models capability flags from providers

title: Z.AI multi-base / CN vs intl config examples
body:
## Goal
Provide clear config examples for Z.AI / Zhipu GLM OpenAI-compat bases (China vs international) and any path quirks.

## Context
Example YAML already has `zai` with a paas base. Operators frequently pick the wrong regional endpoint for their key.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Document CN vs intl base URLs in README or examples comments.
- [ ] Two example provider blocks (or commented alternates) with env key names.
- [ ] Note kind `openai_compat` + optional media capabilities false by default.
- [ ] Link to official docs for confirmation (URLs may change — date the note).

## Implementation notes
- Verify current bases at write time; do not hardcode broken paths without comment.

## Out of scope
- Native non-OpenAI Zhipu protocol.

## Related
- Qwen regional base URL config helpers
- Moonshot token estimate + balance optional proxy routes

title: Qwen regional base URL config helpers
body:
## Goal
Document DashScope/Qwen regional OpenAI-compatible bases (e.g. intl vs cn) and alias patterns for gateway configs.

## Context
Example has `qwen` → `dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1`. Regional mistakes cause auth failures.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Example provider entries for common regions.
- [ ] Notes on model id prefixes and `compatible-mode` path.
- [ ] Optional aliases examples (`qwen-turbo` → `qwen/...`).
- [ ] README provider table or examples section updated.

## Implementation notes
- Docs-first; code helpers only if validation of base_url suffixes is truly useful.

## Out of scope
- DashScope native non-OpenAI APIs.

## Related
- Z.AI multi-base / CN vs intl config examples
- OpenRouter provider routing + plugins docs and passthrough tests

title: Moonshot token estimate + balance optional proxy routes
body:
## Goal
Optionally proxy Moonshot/Kimi helper endpoints (token estimate, balance/quota) used by some clients, or document that only chat/media OpenAI-compat paths are supported.

## Context
Moonshot is configured as openai_compat. Extra routes beyond chat sometimes appear in vendor SDKs.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Investigate current Moonshot OpenAI-compat extra routes; list in issue notes.
- [ ] Either implement thin passthrough under documented paths with provider=moonshot defaults, **or** explicitly wontfix with README pointer.
- [ ] If implemented: air-gapped tests; no special billing math in gateway.
- [ ] Regional bases (`.ai` vs `.cn`) called out.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer generic openai_compat passthrough helper over Moonshot-only hardcoding when possible.

## Out of scope
- Currency conversion of balance.

## Related
- DeepSeek FIM / prefix completion optional routes
- Z.AI multi-base / CN vs intl config examples

title: DeepSeek FIM / prefix completion optional routes
body:
## Goal
Support DeepSeek fill-in-the-middle / beta prefix completion endpoints via optional passthrough routes for coding agent workflows.

## Context
DeepSeek exposes additional completion-style APIs beyond chat. Gateway users pointing base_url at the gateway need these for FIM-capable tools.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Identify current DeepSeek paths (e.g. beta completions/FIM); implement passthrough to openai_compat DeepSeek provider.
- [ ] Model rewrite + auth + usage event (estimated if no usage).
- [ ] Tests with fake upstream.
- [ ] README “provider extras” note; not part of multi-dialect translation.
- [ ] If API unstable, gate behind docs “optional/experimental”.

## Implementation notes
- Do not invent Anthropic/Google FIM twins.

## Out of scope
- Cross-provider FIM translation.

## Related
- Moonshot token estimate + balance optional proxy routes
- xAI Responses + Imagine extras documentation

title: xAI Responses + Imagine extras documentation
body:
## Goal
Document how to use xAI (Grok) through the gateway for Chat, Responses (when M3 lands), and image/Imagine features via openai_compat.

## Context
Example provider `xai` base `https://api.x.ai/v1`. xAI has been expanding toward OpenAI-compatible Responses and image APIs.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Docs: base_url, auth, sample models, aliases (`grok`).
- [ ] Note which gateway routes apply (chat, responses, images) and required `capabilities` for media/realtime.
- [ ] Clarify passthrough-only extras; no xAI-specific IR.
- [ ] Link any header requirements.
- [ ] Example curl/SDK snippets.

## Implementation notes
- Keep docs synced with implemented M3/M4 routes; mark unavailable features clearly.

## Out of scope
- Building xAI-only non-OpenAI protocols.

## Related
- POST /v1/responses passthrough (OpenAI family)
- OpenRouter provider routing + plugins docs and passthrough tests

title: Groq STT-first audio routing examples
body:
## Goal
Provide operator examples for routing speech-to-text to Groq (and similar STT-strong openai_compat hosts) while keeping chat on another provider.

## Context
After M4 audio routes exist, multi-provider configs will use aliases and per-model prefixes. Groq is already in example YAML.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Example YAML: chat default provider ≠ audio transcription models pointing at `groq/...`.
- [ ] `capabilities.audio_transcribe: true` (and speech if applicable) shown for groq openai_compat.
- [ ] Example client calls for OpenAI dialect STT through gateway.
- [ ] Notes on multipart size limits.
- [ ] Links to capability matrix doc.

## Implementation notes
- Docs may land after audio routes merge; otherwise mark as pending routes.

## Out of scope
- Auto-routing by modality without model ids.

## Related
- Anthropic gateway audio speech and transcribe contracts
- Aggregate /v1/models capability flags from providers
- End-to-end compatibility matrix doc in docs/

title: Aggregate GET /v1/models capability flags from providers
body:
## Goal
Implement `GET /v1/models` (roadmap) that lists configured public models/aliases and exposes capability flags so clients can discover what the gateway can route.

## Context
No `/v1/models` today. Aggregation should not require live upstream calls by default (air-gapped, fast, deterministic from YAML + optional static metadata).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `GET /v1/models` returns OpenAI-shaped list object at minimum.
- [ ] Includes aliases and/or `provider/model` forms — document which ids appear.
- [ ] Capability flags per model or per provider (e.g. text/image_gen/video_gen/audio_*/realtime) derived from provider kind defaults + YAML overrides.
- [ ] No mandatory upstream network on this route.
- [ ] Optional future: merge upstream `/models` behind config flag (off by default).
- [ ] Tests with sample config; README + roadmap checkbox.
- [ ] No usage event (discovery) per design §4.7 spirit.

## Implementation notes
- Keep response useful for OpenAI SDK `models.list()`.
- Anthropic/Google discovery can be documented as out of band or same list with metadata.

## Out of scope
- Live pricing.
- Guaranteeing upstream model existence.

## Related
- Capability matrix e2e tests for all modality cells
- End-to-end compatibility matrix doc in docs/
- Provider health check optional

title: Provider health check optional
body:
## Goal
Optional per-provider health/readiness probes for operators (not replacing `/healthz` liveness).

## Context
`/healthz` is local process health. Operators want to know if upstreams are reachable without sending user chat traffic.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Design: either `GET /healthz/providers` admin route (config-gated) or documented external probe patterns.
- [ ] If implemented: timeout-bounded HEAD/GET to provider base; results aggregated; disabled by default.
- [ ] Never logs API keys; uses `api_key_env` if needed for authenticated health endpoints.
- [ ] Tests with httptest up/down.
- [ ] Docs warn about load-balancer misuse (don’t flap on upstream blips without care).

## Implementation notes
- Prefer simple status JSON `{ "openai": "ok", "anthropic": "down" }`.
- Distinct from edge auth: may require edge auth when enabled.

## Out of scope
- Automatic removal of unhealthy providers from routing (canary).

## Related
- Aggregate GET /v1/models capability flags from providers
- Metrics/prometheus optional (or wontfix document)

title: Metrics/Prometheus optional (or wontfix document)
body:
## Goal
Decide whether the gateway exposes Prometheus metrics; implement a minimal endpoint or formally document wontfix in favor of usage hooks + platform metrics.

## Context
Product aims for single static binary, few deps, hooks for billing. Metrics may duplicate hooks. Operators still ask for RED metrics.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] ADR-style note in docs: implement **or** wontfix with rationale.
- [ ] If implement: `GET /metrics` (optional config), counters for requests by dialect/modality/status, WS sessions gauge; no high-cardinality model labels by default.
- [ ] If wontfix: document recommended approach (JSONL hooks → pipeline, sidecars).
- [ ] Tests only if implemented.
- [ ] README ops section updated.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer expvar/prometheus std patterns with optional build tags only if needed; avoid heavy frameworks.

## Out of scope
- Distributed tracing full OpenTelemetry productization (may mention as future).

## Related
- Provider health check optional
- Realtime usage event on session end

title: Request body size / streaming limits review
body:
## Goal
Review and, if needed, adjust body size and streaming limits for media/audio/files workloads; document all limits in one place.

## Context
Current limit 32 MiB request/response; ResponseHeaderTimeout 60s; no overall proxy timeout (streams long-lived). Multimodal and Files may need higher limits or separate multipart limits. Realtime has session minutes.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Audit all limit constants and config knobs.
- [ ] Written table in README: body, header wait, drain, realtime sessions/duration, optional new knobs.
- [ ] Justify keep 32 MiB or make configurable `max_body_bytes`.
- [ ] Streaming: confirm client disconnect cancel still works for media and WS.
- [ ] Tests for oversize body → 413/400 dialect-shaped.
- [ ] SECURITY.md cross-links.

## Implementation notes
- Configurability preferred over silent hardcode changes.
- Consider separate higher limit for multipart only if necessary.

## Out of scope
- CDN upload offload.

## Related
- Multipart + binary response fidelity for TTS
- OpenAI Files API proxy (basic)
- Realtime session limits from config
- Security review of media/audio multipart

title: Security review of media/audio multipart
body:
## Goal
Perform a focused security review of multipart and binary media paths (images edits, STT, files) for memory exhaustion, temp file leaks, log leakage, and SSRF via URL-based image inputs.

## Context
Design §14: no persistence beyond request lifetime; no body logging; key_hash only. Multipart peeks for model. Translation paths may parse base64 images.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Written review notes (docs or issue comment → linked from SECURITY.md).
- [ ] Checklist: size limits, decompression bombs, boundary abuse, filename paths, content-type confusion.
- [ ] Confirm gateway does not fetch arbitrary image URLs server-side unless intentional; if it does, SSRF controls documented/fixed.
- [ ] Tests for oversized multipart and malicious filenames where applicable.
- [ ] Remediation issues filed for any findings (or fixed in same PR).

## Implementation notes
- Prefer fail closed on ambiguous multipart parse errors.
- Coordinate with edge auth (does not replace upstream key validity).

## Out of scope
- Full pen-test of deploy manifests.
- Provider-side content safety.

## Related
- Request body size / streaming limits review
- Optional gateway edge auth (API key gate)
- OpenAI Files API proxy (basic)

title: Changelog + versioning for API surface expansion
body:
## Goal
Establish a clear changelog and versioning practice as M3/M4 expand the public HTTP/WS surface so operators know when clients must upgrade.

## Context
Release flow uses `v*` tags and GitHub Releases. Media Contract v1 and Responses/Realtime are semver-significant for consumers even if Go module path is stable.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` (or release template) with categories: Added/Changed/Fixed/Security.
- [ ] Policy: minor for additive routes; major for breaking wire changes; patch for fixes.
- [ ] Document Gateway Media Contract v1 versioning relative to gateway semver.
- [ ] PR checklist item in CONTRIBUTING for changelog entries.
- [ ] Note deprecation policy link (related issue).

## Implementation notes
- Keep format Keep-a-Changelog compatible.

## Out of scope
- Automated changelog from commits (nice-to-have).

## Related
- Document Gateway Media Contract v1 in README
- Deprecation policy for silent field drops (warn header?)
- CONTRIBUTING guide for adding a modality

title: CONTRIBUTING guide for adding a modality
body:
## Goal
Extend CONTRIBUTING.md with a step-by-step guide for adding a new modality (HTTP or WS) consistent with capability-centric design and TDD air-gapped rules.

## Context
Design §12 and package layout define the intended extension points: capability flags, ingress/egress adapters, canonical types, proxy handlers, fixtures, matrix cells, usage events.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Sections: when to passthrough vs translate; capability defaults; config fields; hooks fields; test matrix requirements; fixture naming.
- [ ] Checklist matching design §12.13 Definition of Done.
- [ ] Pointers to example PRs/issues (image_gen, realtime).
- [ ] Explicit ban list: live network tests in CI, inventing fake Anthropic Realtime, logging bodies/keys.
- [ ] Layout table updated for `canonical/*`, `proxy/realtime`, media handlers.

## Implementation notes
- Keep tone aligned with existing CONTRIBUTING brevity; link out to design spec for depth.

## Out of scope
- Rewriting the full design spec.

## Related
- Package split canonical/{chat,image,video,audio,realtime}.go
- Capability matrix e2e tests for all modality cells
- Changelog + versioning for API surface expansion

title: End-to-end compatibility matrix doc in docs/
body:
## Goal
Publish a human-readable compatibility matrix under `docs/` covering dialect × modality × provider kind, reflecting implemented reality and linking to automated tests.

## Context
Design §6 and §12.6 are normative for implementers; operators need a simpler matrix. Should not drift from tests — document how CI enforces cells.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `docs/compatibility-matrix.md` (or similar) with tables for text, image_gen, video_gen, audio_*, realtime.
- [ ] Legend: native passthrough / translated / unsupported.
- [ ] openai_compat opt-in called out.
- [ ] Link from README.
- [ ] Note last-updated date and owning tests package path.
- [ ] Mentions hermetic e2e; no claim of live vendor certification unless SDK matrix issue covers it.

## Implementation notes
- Generate from comments/tests later if desired; hand-maintained ok with checklist in CONTRIBUTING.

## Out of scope
- Marketing comparison of model quality.

## Related
- Capability matrix e2e tests for all modality cells
- SDK compatibility test matrix (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google official SDKs hermetic)
- Document Gateway Media Contract v1 in README

title: Claude Code regression checklist
body:
## Goal
Create a regression checklist ensuring Claude Code and Anthropic SDK workflows keep working as the gateway grows Responses/media/realtime surfaces.

## Context
Anthropic passthrough for messages is a flagship path (`examples/claude-code.sh`). Stream token caveats and count_tokens estimate behavior already exist. New routes must not break `/v1/messages` headers, SSE, tools, or beta features.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Checklist doc: config, env, base URL, model routing, streaming, tools, count_tokens, prompt caching betas, files (when present).
- [ ] Manual steps + automated tests that cover critical paths (link test names).
- [ ] Notes on translated path (Anthropic→OpenAI-compat) known limitations.
- [ ] Runbook for release sign-off before tagging.
- [ ] Linked from README examples and CONTRIBUTING release section.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer expanding hermetic tests over requiring live Claude Code in CI.
- Include header beta forwarding verification.

## Out of scope
- Shipping Claude Code itself.

## Related
- Forward anthropic-beta unknown values (verify + harden)
- Anthropic Files API proxy (beta headers forward)
- SDK compatibility test matrix (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google official SDKs hermetic)

title: SDK compatibility test matrix (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google official SDKs hermetic)
body:
## Goal
Define and implement a hermetic compatibility strategy for official OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google SDKs against the gateway with fake upstreams (no live keys in CI).

## Context
Go tests cover wire contracts; SDK clients may send additional headers, serialization quirks, or multipart forms. Full SDK installs in CI may be heavy — choose practical approach (recorded fixtures, subprocess tests, or documented manual matrix).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Document matrix: SDK × dialect routes × expected pass.
- [ ] At least one automated hermetic path per dialect proving critical happy path (may be Go net/http mimicking SDK defaults if full SDK too heavy).
- [ ] If using real SDKs: isolated job or optional make target; still air-gapped via fake upstream.
- [ ] No `-tags live` in default CI.
- [ ] Results linked from compatibility matrix doc.

## Implementation notes
- Start with HTTP-level assertions of headers SDKs require (anthropic-version, user-agent tolerance, content-types).
- Pin SDK versions if introduced.

## Out of scope
- Certifying every SDK minor release automatically.
- Mobile SDKs.

## Related
- End-to-end compatibility matrix doc in docs/
- Claude Code regression checklist
- Capability matrix e2e tests for all modality cells

title: Deprecation policy for silent field drops (warn header?)
body:
## Goal
Define how the gateway handles dropping unmapped fields during cross-dialect translation: metrics, tests, optional warning headers, and deprecation timelines — avoiding silent data loss surprises.

## Context
Design acknowledges non-lossless media translation; Realtime bridge has explicit drop lists. Chat translation already drops some vendor-specific fields. Operators and agents need observability when fields vanish.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Written policy: passthrough never drops; translation may drop with explicit lists in tests.
- [ ] Decision on `Warning` HTTP header or custom `x-gateway-dropped-fields` (yes/no + format).
- [ ] Decision on hooks/metrics counters for drops.
- [ ] Semver/changelog rules when drop behavior changes.
- [ ] Examples for image Extra fields and realtime unmapped events.
- [ ] Implement minimal enforcement if policy requires header/metric; otherwise document and file follow-ups.

## Implementation notes
- Prefer explicit drop lists in code next to translators.
- Do not spam huge headers with full payloads.

## Out of scope
- Guaranteeing zero drops forever.

## Related
- Changelog + versioning for API surface expansion
- Epic: Optional Realtime ↔ Live bridge IR + subtasks
- Cross-dialect image gen translation canonical
- Rate-limit header passthrough policy
